rulings.Case Title: PHILIP CHANT et al VS. FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of (1) Rafael Barrientos; (2) A Properly Designated Person Most Knowledgeable Re: Parking Tickets Incurred By Drivers Who Operated Vhicles Out Of Fedes Terminal 941 For The Periof Of Aug 2007 - Apr 30, 2008; (3) A Porperly Designated Person Most Knowledgable Re: Motor Vehicles In Ca: Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $6,080
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 25, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFFs PHILIP CHANT, LAURA CHANT, ANDREW CHANT, MASON CHAN'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of (1) Rafael Barrientos; (2) A Properly Designated Person Most Knowledgeable Re: Parking Tickets Incurred By Drivers Who Operated Vehicles Out Of Fedex Terminal 941 For The Period Of Aug 2007 - Apr 30, 2008; (3) A Properly Designated Person Most Knowledgable Re: Motor Vehicles In Ca: Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $6,080. Motion is granted in part. Plaintiff can depose Cook. Motion denied as to remaining requests. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. JOEY AND EDDIE'S, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 25, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment is continued to September 2, 2010. Opposing party to provide a courtesy copy to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FAHD ALKARSHI VS. ABDUL BASET HUBAISHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Fahd Alkarshi'S Motion For Summary Adjudication (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Aug-4-2010)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 25, 2010, line 1. Plaintiff Fahd Alkarshi's Motion For Summary Adjudication is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of proof because plaintiff does not present any competent evidence regarding the amount of monetary damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRED JACKMAN VS. LEONID TOLSTUNOV DDS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 25, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT LEONID TOLSTUNOV'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action for breach of contract. No express contract provided, no offer or acceptance properly alleged. See McKinney v. Nash (1981) 120 Cal App 3d 428. This action is stayed pending resolution of the small claims case. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOSE GARAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Order Permitting Late Claim Against Governmental Entity [Gov.C 946.6]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 25, 2010, line 9. PETITIONERS JOSE GARAY, NANCY GARAY'S Petition For Order Permitting Late Claim Against Governmental Entity is granted. G.C. section 946.6 is liberally construed so as to have cases heard on ther merits. Draper v. City of Los Angeles (1990) 52 Cal 3d 502. Petitioners demonstrated that Bell exercised reasonable diligence in investigating petitioners' claim and Respondents fail to offer evidence of prejudice. =(302/CWW)
